KAISER FAILED TO MEET AND CONFER WITH PLAINTIFF REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES TO STANDARD INTERROGATORIES, WHICH WERE SERVED PRIOR TO KAISER FILING THIS MOTION. C.C.P. 2016.040. ASBESTOS DISCOVERY 503, DEFENDANT KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS, SET THREE, AND FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET THREE. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS AMENDED: KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, SET THREE, AND FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET THREE, IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS' AMENDED RESPONSES SERVED AUGUST 8, 2012 ARE DEFICIENT. PLAINTIFFS' TIMELINESS OBJECTION THAT THE MOTION WAS NOT FILED WITHIN 45 DAYS OF SERVICE OF PLAINTIFFS' RESPONSES IS OVERRULED. THE MOTION PERTAINS TO PLAINTIFFS' AMENDED RESPONSES SERVED AUGUST 8, 2012, MAKING THE MOTION TIMELY. PLAINTIFFS' TIMELINESS OBJECTION THAT THE MOTION IS BEING HEARD WITHIN 15 DAYS OF THE TRIAL DATE AND THAT DISCOVERY IS CLOSED IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT OBTAINED A COURT ORDER ALLOWING THIS MOTION TO BE HEARD ON THIS DATE AND SUCH ORDER REASONABLY ALLOWS THE COURT TO RULE ON THE MOTION, INCLUDING A RULING GRANTING THE MOTION AND COMPELLING PLAINTIFFS TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES. PLAINTIFFS' FURTHER RESPONSES WILL BE DUE BY 9/19 IF CASE IS ASSIGNED TO TRIAL ON 9/17. IF THE CASE IS NOT ASSIGNED TO TRIAL THEN PLAINTIFFS HAVE UNTIL 9/27 TO RESPOND. (C.C.P. 2024.020, 2024.050.) COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT TO PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: PATRICIA RODRIGUEZ OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT BW/IP, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN UNDER EITHER PRONG OF AGUILAR. DEFENDANT FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFFS DO NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT MR. PATTERSON WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT IS PRECLUDED FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR BECAUSE DEFENDANT FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFFS SUBMITTED FACTUALLY DEVOID SUBSTANTIVE RESPONSES, AS OPPOSED TO ONLY OBJECTIONS, TO CONTENTION DISCOVERY DESIGNED TO ELICIT ALL OF PLAINTIFFS' EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF THEIR CLAIMS AGAINST DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT FAILED TO NEGATE AN ESSENTIAL ELEMENT OF PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS. PLAINTIFFS' OBJECTIONS TO MR. COSTANZO'S DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED. EVEN IF ADMISSIBLE, MR. COSTANZO'S DECLARATION DOES NOT NEGATE AN ESSENTIAL ELEMENT OF PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS. THE COURT ONLY CONSIDERED THIS AS A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANT'S CAPTION TITLES IT A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, DEFENDANT'S NOTICE AND SEPARATE STATEMENT FAILED TO STATE THE SPECIFIC CAUSES OF ACTION OR ISSUES SOUGHT TO BE ADJUDICATED IN VIOLATION OF CRC 3.1350(B), (D). EVEN IF THE COURT CONSIDERS DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT IN SUPPORT OF ITS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT TO APPLY TO ISSUES SOUGHT TO BE SUMMARILY ADJUDICATED, DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT DID NOT MAKE AN ISSUE OF FALSE REPRESENTATION, INTENTIONAL TORT, LOSS OF CONSORTIUM OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: SANDRA SUSANA PETRITSCH, CSR #11684

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, PLAINTIFFS GEORGE PATTERSON & SHERLEY PATTERSON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS' AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES IS GRANTED AS TO DEFENDANT'S 1ST THROUGH 3RD, 5TH THROUGH 16TH, AND 19TH THROUGH 25TH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES AND DENIED AS TO DEFENDANT'S 4TH, 17TH, 18TH AND 26TH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. AS TO THE 1ST THROUGH 3RD, 5TH THROUGH 16TH, AND 19TH THROUGH 25TH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, PLAINTIFFS SUSTAINED THEIR INITIAL BURDEN AND DEFENDANT FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER ANY OF THOSE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES APPLY. AS TO THE 4TH, 17TH, 18TH AND 26TH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, PLAINTIFFS SUSTAINED THEIR INITIAL BURDEN AND PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT AND RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S CONTENTION DISCOVERY CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER THOSE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES APPLY. ALTHOUGH UNVERIFIED, PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT MAY BE CONSIDERED. PLAINTIFFS' OBJECTIONS TO ITS RESPONSES TO DEFENDANTS' SPECIAL DISCOVERY ARE OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S OPPOSITION WAS TIMELY AND WAS CONSIDERED BY THE COURT. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS TO PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: JENNIFER SEELENBACHER OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11878

ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS MET THE REQUIREMENTS FOR RELIEF UNDER CCP SECTIONS 2030.290, 2031.300 AND 2033.280. DEFENDANT, A DISSOLVED CORPORATION, IS UNDER NO OBLIGATION TO PROVIDE VERIFICATIONS. (MALDONADO V. SUPERIOR COURT (2002) 94 CAL.APP.4TH 1390.) THE PARTIES STIPULATE THAT RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY REQUESTS ARE DEEMED VERIFIED. THE COURT DOES NOT AWARD SANCTIONS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, NOT REPORTED

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT DILLINGHAM CONSTRUCTION CORP.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT PLAINTIFFS DO NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT MR. PATTERSON WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT IS PRECLUDED FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR BECAUSE DEFENDANT FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFFS SUBMITTED FACTUALLY DEVOID SUBSTANTIVE RESPONSES, AS OPPOSED TO ONLY OBJECTIONS, TO CONTENTION DISCOVERY DESIGNED TO ELICIT ALL OF PLAINTIFFS' EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF THEIR CLAIMS AGAINST DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT DID NOT ATTEMPT TO SUSTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN UNDER THE NEGATION OF AN ELEMENT PRONG OF AGUILAR. PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. ORDER WAS SIGNED AFTER THE HEARING. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Depositions Of The Person Most Qualified And Custodian Of Records Of Defendant Cahill Construction Co., Inc., Or In The Alternative, Motion For An Order Imposing Terminating Sanctions, Monetary Sanctions, And Evidentiary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
J. Bruce Jackson of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (510-844-5500), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties George & Sherley Patterson
Cesar A. Alvarado of ARCHER NORRIS (925-930-6600), appearing for defendants / opposing parties John Calhill, Jr., Archer Norris and Cahill Construction Co., Inc.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS GEORGE & SHERLEY PATTERSON'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITIONS OF THE PERSON MOST QUALIFIED AND CUSTODIAN OF RECORDS OF DEFENDANT CAHILL CONSTRUCTION CO., INC., OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR AN ORDER IMPOSING TERMINATING SANCTIONS, MONETARY SANCTIONS, AND EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS

Hearing held. The Court ruled as follows: Plaintiffs' motion to compel the depositions of the person most qualified and custodian of records of defendant Cahill Construction Co., Inc., or in the alternative, motion to impose sanctions is denied per the Maldonado case. Prevailing party to prepare an order. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Laura Martinez CSR # 11332 (Phone: 415-250-2926) (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Requiring John Cahill, Jr., And Archer Norris Aplc To Pay The Costs And Fees For The Failure To Appear At Deposition
Off Calendar Mar-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
J. Bruce Jackson of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (510-844-5500), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties George & Sherley Patterson
Cesar A. Alvarado of ARCHER NORRIS (925-930-6600), appearing for defendants / opposing parties John Calhill, Jr., Archer Norris and Cahill Construction Co., Inc.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS GEORGE PATTERSON & SHERLEY PATTERSON'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER REQUIRING JOHN CAHILL, JR., AND ARCHER NORRIS APLC TO PAY THE COSTS AND FEES FOR THE FAILURE TO APPEAR AT DEPOSITION

Hearing held and the Court adopted its tentative ruling issued on 3/6/2013: Plaintiffs' motion for an order requiring John Cahill, Jr. and Archer Norris to pay the costs and fees for the failure to appear at deposition is granted. There is no substantial justification why Mr. Cahill failed to appear at deposition pursuant to a subpoena. Sanctions of $500 are awarded against Mr. Cahill. Sanctions of $3,203 are awarded against Archer Norris. The initial opposition of Cahill Construction Company, Inc. was timely. Sanctions to be paid to plaintiffs within 30 days. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Laura Martinez CSR # 11332 (Phone: 415-250-2926) (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Cahill Construction Co., Inc.S Further Verified Discovery Responses And Deeming Plaintiffs Requests For Admission Admitted; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
J. Bruce Jackson of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (510-844-5500), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties George & Sherley Patterson
Cesar A. Alvarado of ARCHER NORRIS (925-930-6600), appearing for defendants / opposing parties John Calhill, Jr., Archer Norris and Cahill Construction Co., Inc.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS GEORGE & SHERLEY PATTERSON'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT CAHILL CONSTRUCTION CO., INC.'S FURTHER VERIFIED DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND DEEMING PLAINTIFFS REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION ADMITTED; AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to compel defendant Cahill Construction Co., Inc. to provide further responses to plaintiffs' special interrogatories nos. 27-28, 55-57, 73-74, 77-98 and requests for production nos. 9, 13-16, 18-21, 24, 34, 36-41, 46, 48, 51, 53, 55, 56-65 is granted. Defendant's responses are inadequate. Defendant has 15 days to serve supplemental responses. Plaintiffs' motion to compel defendant to verify its responses is denied. Defendant, a dissolved corporation, is under no obligation to provide verifications. (Maldonado v. Superior Court (2002) 94 Cal.App.4th 1390.) Since defendant is not required to provide verifications, defendant responded to plaintiffs' requests for admission so such requests will not be deemed admitted. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is granted in part and denied in part. Defendant is sanctioned in the amount of $1330 to be paid within 30 days of this order. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Laura Martinez CSR # 11332 (Phone: 415-250-2926) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-11-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Cesar A. Alvarado of ARCHER NORRIS (925-930-6600), appearing for defendant / moving party Cahill Construction Services, Inc.
Stephen M. Fishback of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (818-342-7442), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties George & Sherley Patterson

RULING:
DEFENDANT CAHILL CONSTRUCTION SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Cahill Construction Services, Inc.'s Motion for summary is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Patterson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. By failing to attach plaintiffs' verifications to defendant's contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) Defendant did not satisfy its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims against defendant. Even if admissible, the Articles of Incorporation do not demonstrate that defendant did not work at any site where Mr. Patterson worked. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Sep-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jeffrey T. Thayer of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0750), appearing for defendant / moving party Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.
J. Bruce Jackson of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (415-775-1206), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties George Patterson, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.'s MSA of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Specifically, plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories identified Coy Yancy as a person with facts supporting plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages (no. 32), provided his address and defendant's moving papers failed to demonstrate that Mr. Yancy did not know facts supporting plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages. Defendant's special interrogatories did not ask what facts or knowledge persons identified in response to that interrogatory had. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

DEFENDANT CRANE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

As to issues 1, 2 and 3 (punitive damages, false representation and intentional tort), defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting plaintiffs' false representation and intentional tort causes of action and claim for punitive damages. By failing to attach plaintiffs' verifications to defendant's contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) Adjudication of issue 4 (loss of consortium) is denied pursuant to VanHooser v. Superior Court (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 921. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Janell M. Alberto of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for / moving party defendant BW/IP, Inc.
Wes Wagnon of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (415-775-1206), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties George Patterson, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT BW/IP, INC. AND IT'S WHOLLY OWNED SUBSIDIARIES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant BW/IP, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Patterson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid substantive responses, as opposed to only objections, to contention discovery designed to elicit all of plaintiffs' evidence in support of their claims against defendant. Defendant failed to negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Plaintiffs' objections to Mr. Costanzo's declaration are sustained. Even if admissible, Mr. Costanzo's declaration does not negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. The court only considered this as a motion for summary judgment. Although defendant's caption titles it a motion for summary judgment or in the alternative motion for summary adjudication, defendant's notice and separate statement failed to state the specific causes of action or issues sought to be adjudicated in violation of CRC 3.1350(b), (d). Even if the court considers defendant's separate statement in support of its motion for summary judgment to apply to issues sought to be summarily adjudicated, defendant's separate statement did not make an issue of false representation, intentional tort, loss of consortium or punitive damages. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Sandra Susana Petritsch, CSR #11684 (503/TLJ)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-15-2011 Continued To Jul-20-11 At 09:15 A.M. In Dept 503
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Jennifer Walker of Brown Eassa & McLeod (510-444-3131 for Dow Chemical Company Defendant and Moving Party / Wes Wagnon of Keller, Fishback & Jackson LLP (818-342-7442) for Plaintiffs and Opposing Parties

RULING:
DEFENDANT THE DOW CHEMICAL COMPANY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

Argued and Tentative Ruling Adopted:

Defendant Dow Chemical's demurrer to plaintiff's complaint is continued on the court's own motion to July 20. Assuming without deciding that defendant's demurrer is untimely, the court has the discretion to decide it on its merits. McAllister v. County of Monterey, 147 Cal. App. 4th 253, 281. Since plaintiffs did not file a substantive opposition, the court is continuing the hearing to allow plaintiffs to do so, if they wish. Plaintiff's opposition, if any, must be filed and served by July 6 at 4:00pm. Defendant's reply, if any, must be filed and served by July 13 at 4:00pm. JUDGE TERI L. JACKSON, CSR:T. WILLAT-HARRISON, #4609 = (503/TLJ)

DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-15-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Wes Wagnon of Keller Fishback & Jackson LLP (818-342-7442), for Plaintiff and Moving Party / Jann Noddin of Vasquez Estrada & Conway LLP (415-453-0555) for Hills Brothers Chemical Co., Defendant and Opposing Party

RULING:
Plaintiffs' demurrer to defendant Hill Brothers Chemical Co.'s answer to plaintiffs' complaint

Argued and Tentative Ruling Adopted:
Plaintiffs' demurrer is overruled as to the 26 affirmative defenses to which they demurred. Given the lack of factual specificity of the allegations in the complaint against defendant, it is not reasonable to expect defendant to plead its affirmative defenses with greater factual specificity. Order signed and filed in open court. JUDGE TERI L. JACKSON, CSR:T. WILLAT-HARRISON, #4609
